steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Mulier: 'Mulier' is a noun meaning 'woman' — the subject
hints.spiritum: 'Spiritum' is a noun meaning 'spirit' in the accusative
hints.infirmitatis: 'Infirmitatis' is a noun meaning 'of infirmity' in the genitive

steps.reveal: habebat = she was having / she had (ongoing)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'habet' — 'habebat' has the -ba- imperfect marker

hints.habito, habitare, habitavi, habitatum: These are the parts of 'habitare' (to dwell)
hints.abeo, abire, abii, abitum: These are the parts of 'abire' (to go away)
hints.debeo, debere, debui, debitum: These are the parts of 'debere' (to owe)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'spiritus' — 'spiritum' with -um is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'spiritus' (with long -ūs) — 'spiritum' is accusative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'spiritus' has gen. in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'spiritus' has gen. in -ūs
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'spiritus' has gen. in -ūs

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'infirmitas' — 'infirmitatis' with -is is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'infirmitatis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'infirmitatis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'infirmitatis' ends in -is

steps.reveal: annis decem et octo = for eighteen years (abl.)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'anni' — 'annis' is dative/ablative
hints.dative: 'Annis' could be dative, but here it expresses duration — ablative of time
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'annorum' — 'annis' is ablative

translation: A woman who had a spirit of infirmity for eighteen years
